name: trivy
description: A simple and comprehensive vulnerability scanner

name: --severity
-s
subcommands.description: Severities of vulnerabilities to be displayed (comma separated) (default: "UNKNOWN,LOW,MEDIUM,HIGH,CRITICAL") [$TRIVY_SEVERITY]

subcommands.name: --exit-code
subcommands.description: Exit code when vulnerabilities were found (default: 0) [$TRIVY_EXIT_CODE]

subcommands.name: --security-checks
subcommands.description: Comma-separated list of what security issues to detect (vuln,config) (default: "vuln") [$TRIVY_SECURITY_CHECKS]

subcommands.name: --ignore-policy
subcommands.description: Specify the Rego file to evaluate each vulnerability [$TRIVY_IGNORE_POLICY]

subcommands.name: --list-all-pkgs
subcommands.description: Enabling the option will output all packages regardless of vulnerability (default: false) [$TRIVY_LIST_ALL_PKGS]

subcommands.name: --offline-scan
subcommands.description: Do not issue API requests to identify dependencies [$TRIVY_OFFLINE_SCAN]

subcommands.name: --skip-files
subcommands.description: Specify the file paths to skip traversal [$TRIVY_SKIP_FILES]

subcommands.name: --skip-dirs
subcommands.description: Allow insecure server connections when using SSL [$TRIVY_INSECURE]

subcommands.name: --removed-pkgs
subcommands.description: Detect vulnerabilities of removed packages (default: false) [$TRIVY_REMOVED_PKGS]

name: --input
-i
subcommands.description: Input file path instead of image name [$TRIVY_INPUT]

subcommands.name: --download-db-only
subcommands.description: Download/update vulnerability database but don't run a scan [$TRIVY_DOWNLOAD_DB_ONLY]

subcommands.name: --insecure
subcommands.description: Allow insecure server connections when using SSL [$TRIVY_INSECURE]

name: filesystem
fs
subcommands.description: Scan local filesystem for language-specific dependencies and config files

subcommands.name: --config-policy

subcommands.name: --config-data
subcommands.description: Specify paths from which data for the Rego policies will be recursively loaded [$TRIVY_CONFIG_DATA]

subcommands.name: --token-header
subcommands.description: Specify a header name for token in client/server mode (default: "Trivy-Token") [$TRIVY_TOKEN_HEADER]
